channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Graffiti at entrance to the borough, 14th May
item.description:
Please paint these properties in dark colours or prevent them being repeatedly covered in graffiti.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Brockley Way, Honor Oak, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping - still present, 14th May
item.description:
Previously reported, marked as fixed but it’s not. A chair, suitcase and picture frame that have been there two weeks.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 244, Underhill Road, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Notices on lamp posts, 14th May
item.description:
Notices have been attached to several lamp posts down the road about skid risk/speed 10 mph. They are low on the post which is dangerous to pedestrians. The posters seem to be appearing all over the borough
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Felton Close, Petts Wood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rats and filthy conditions, 14th May
item.description:
I posted some pictures yesterday of Burrell Row off Beckenham High Street, because of the filthy conditions, and today I see more rubbish dumped there. This is being dumped by the residents who have moved in the flats. This is absolutely disgusting. Bromley council you need to deal with this as an emergency. You got restaurants galore in the high street and this will generate rats, mice cockroach’s and foxes. It’s vermin. There ia dirty nappies food and human feces dirty underwear, this will cause diseases. If this is not dealt with I will be notifying the restaurants, I will post these pictures on the Beckenham appreciation Group and I will also notify the local Newspaper. It’s a disgrace. Beckenham is not a dumping ground. What have you done to this town ? I see this sort of behavior everywhere in Beckenham these days. I’ve have lived in Beckenham for many many years and I have never seen the streets of Beckenham like it is today! Shame on you Bromley Council. You need reporting to health and safety!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Village Way, Eden Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rainwater gulleys on both sides of Avenue road blocked causing backup to cover the road and pavements, 14th May
item.description:
For the past two days of rain the rainwater gulleys outside 197 Avenue road, and the one opposite have become clogged with debris so they are not draining
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 194, Avenue Road, Northumberland Heath, Erith, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped television, 14th May
item.description:
Television dumped on the pathway . I moved it to the side as it is a public footpath .

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Milton Road, Lessness Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flyposting on traffic sign (Kennington LU), 14th May
item.description:
Unauthorised/illegal flyposting on traffic island sign

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kennington Park Road, Elephant and Castle, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flyposting on traffic sign, 14th May
item.description:
Unauthorised/illegal posting of ads

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 324, Kennington Lane, Vauxhall, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Remains of BBQ, 14th May
item.description:
Damage to grass from 'illegal?' Bbq. Large burned area with lots of broken glass and debris. Danger to people, children, dogs, wildlife and needs to be cleared asap. Has been here for a week or more now. Thank you...concerned park user, and dog walker

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Danson Park, Welling, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown trees hanging over foot path, 14th May
item.description:
Obstructing use of the foot path

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Red Hill, Chislehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 2 x cars blocking pavement, 14th May
item.description:
2 x cars blocking pavement
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Farnham Road, East Wickham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken lamp post, 14th May
item.description:
The lamp cover on the post on Park Crescent near the junction with Bexley Road is hanging open. It's not clear if it is broken with potential to fall on a member of the public. The light itself still works.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Park Crescent, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytip on pavement, 14th May
item.description:
Pallets, radiator and frame on pavement

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 52, Lant Street, The Borough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Blocked drainhole, 14th May
item.description:
This is a long time problem

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 181, Sidney Road, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping on pavement, 14th May
item.description:
Discovered on Tues 12th May.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Clapton Way, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: There are always piles of flytipped rubbish and furniture at this spot, 14th May
item.description:
There is regularly piles of flytipped furniture dumped at this spot on Edgehill Road. Please can the flytips be cleared and this area be monitored. It looks like the patch has become a dumping ground for the local flats to get rid of any rubbish - and I am unclear why the council don’t seem to be taking any action. Please can this area, and the grass area of Edgehill be cleared of rubbish and then monitored to prevent more build up.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Plumstead Common Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Sofa under stairwell, 14th May
item.description:
Someone has flytipped a sofa underneath the stairwell of this block. So 15B Mundania Road is up the block of stairs, the sofa is tucked away under the stairwell

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Mundania Road, Nunhead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Two electric bikes with batteries attached dumped on the bottom of the stairs, 14th May
item.description:
Two electric bikes with batteries attached dumped on the bottom of the stairs, area that gets flooded with tides, just by the Bankside pier. Battery leaking danger and access to the beach is hampered.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Southwark, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Daily fly tipping, 14th May
item.description:
Daily fly tipping at this location

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Mackenzie Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Blocking pavement, 14th May
item.description:
Rubbish pile

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Mina Road, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
